channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Unesco commits to strengthen Tanzania higher education with focus on gender equality in ICT
item.description: The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (Unesco) has promised to keep working with the Tanzanian government to improve higher education systems across the country. The mai...

item.title: ACT Conservation Council Expresses Concern Over Delay in Urban Growth Boundary
item.description: The head of the ACT Conservation Council, Simon Copland, has expressed disappointment over the lack of progress on a hard urban growth boundary for Canberra, almost a year after the government pledged...

item.title: Nagawang Grade 9 student sa Pasig, nadiskubreng patay sa pribadong compound
item.description: Nadiskubreng patay ang 15-taong‑gulang na Grade 9 student na wala na ng ilang araw sa isang pribadong compound sa Barangay Santolan, Pasig City noong Lunes. Ayon sa pulisya, ang huling pagkakakita sa ...

item.title: Joburg launches pilot electric vehicle charging stations to cut fuel costs and support cleaner transport
item.description: The City of Johannesburg has officially launched its first electric vehicle (EV) charging stations, signalling a major step towards cleaner transport and reduced fuel expenditure. Mayor Dada Morero an...

item.title: Joseph and Kendra Duggar arrested on child endangerment and false imprisonment charges following molestation allegations
item.description: Joseph Duggar, a former star of the reality television series 19 Kids and Counting, was arrested in Arkansas on 18 March 2026 in connection with allegations of lewd and lascivious behaviour involving ...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Escalates as Oil Prices Fluctuate
item.description: The ongoing Middle East conflict, now in its fourth week, has seen significant developments, including threats from former US President Donald Trump to strike Iran's power plants if Tehran fails to re...

item.title: Experts Warn of Potential Dangers from Leaking Radioactive Tomb in the Pacific
item.description: The Runit Dome, a structure built by the United States military on the Marshall Islands in the 1970s, has been leaking radioactive material for decades. Initially created to contain waste from nuclear...

item.title: Tanzania's Universities Plan to Tackle AI Challenges and Risks
item.description: Tanzania is positioning its universities as the key players in managing both the opportunities and risks posed by artificial intelligence (AI). With advancements in AI bringing transformative change, ...

item.title: EACC Holds High-Level Dialogue on Strengthening Justice Sector to Combat Corruption
item.description: The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) is holding a major dialogue this week in Nakuru, bringing together top justice sector institutions to assess Kenya's progress in fighting corruption an...

item.title: Taliban releases US researcher Dennis Coyle after over a year in detention
item.description: The Afghan Taliban authorities have announced the release of Dennis Coyle, a US citizen who had been detained in Afghanistan since January 2025. Coyle, aged 64, is a linguist and academic researcher w...

item.title: Tabora launches campaign to vaccinate over 1 million children against polio
item.description: In Tabora, more than 1 million children under the age of 10 will soon receive the polio vaccine. The vaccination campaign started on March 24, 2026, and will conclude by March 27, 2026. The event took...

item.title: Minnesota files federal lawsuit against Trump administration for allegedly withholding evidence in shootings involving Renee Good and Alex Pretti
item.description: Minnesota officials have launched a federal lawsuit against the Trump administration, accusing it of failing to cooperate with state investigations into three shootings carried out by federal officers...

item.title: KeNHA Seeks Firms for National Expressway Masterplan Development
item.description: The Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA) has called on consulting firms to express their interest in preparing an Expressway Network Masterplan for the country. This comes after the Kenyan govern...

item.title: Philippines fuel supply drops to 45 days average amid Middle East conflict, says Energy Secretary Garin
item.description: Energy Secretary Sharon Garin announced that as of March 20, 2026, the Philippines maintains an average fuel supply of 45 days, which is 10 days lower than the 55 to 57 days recorded when the war in t...

item.title: Ultimum opens new beverage plant in Aba, boosting local manufacturing and job creation
item.description: Ultimum Limited, makers of the Razzl brand of carbonated soft drinks, has opened a new manufacturing plant in Aba, Abia State, which aligns with the state’s push for industrialization. The new facilit...

item.title: Australians Petition Against Public Funding for Harry and Meghan's Private Visit
item.description: A petition has gained traction in Australia calling for Prince Harry and Meghan Markle to fund their upcoming visit privately, rather than relying on taxpayer money. The campaign, led by the advocacy ...

item.title: Former Zutons guitarist Boyan Chowdhury injured in racist assault in Liverpool
item.description: Boyan Chowdhury, a founding member and former guitarist of the Liverpool indie band The Zutons, was seriously injured in a racist attack in the Wavertree area of Liverpool on Saturday afternoon. The i...

item.title: US-Cuba tensions escalate amid fuel shortages and international pressure
item.description: On 16 March 2026, Cuba’s national electricity grid collapsed for the third time in four months, leaving 10 million people in darkness for over 29 hours. Hospitals struggled to operate, water supply wa...

item.title: Iran's Key Demands for Ending US Conflict Emerge Amid Indirect Talks and Trump's Claims
item.description: As the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran continues, fresh signals from Tehran indicate that any negotiations for truce will come with strict and possibly non-negotiable conditions. U...

item.title: Five legal professionals shot in the past 10 months, highlighting safety concerns in the legal field
item.description: In the past 10 months, five legal professionals have been shot, with four of them fatally. The most recent case was that of Chinette Gallichan, a labour lawyer who was killed outside the Commission fo...
